stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of vehicle speed using a speedometer. The measurements have a mean of 38.6 km/h and a standard deviation of 4.3 km/h. However, calibration tests reveal that a speedometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 1.1% and 3.4%. Estimate the true vehicle speed and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 38.6 km/h. After correcting for the 2.2% bias: ≈ 39.5 km/h. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.1%-3.4%) and sampling variability: [37.4, 41.6]. Point estimate: 39.5 km/h.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
